It was compiled in 833 CE ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō}} 10) under an imperial edict from Emperor Junna, with the right minister {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|1=Kiyohara no Natsuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560500}} serving as chief editor. The compilation involved 12 notable scholars, including {{ill|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|en|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|WD=Q3503077|lt=Sugawara no Kiyotomo}}, {{ill|Sanuki no Emanari|ja|讃岐永直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki no Emanari|1=Sanuki no Naganao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632551}}, and others<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=令義解序|publisher= |series=新訂増補 国史大系|volume=第22|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431635/1/118|author=黒板}}</ref>. The work originally comprised 30 articles across 10 volumes<ref name=":0" />, but only 21 articles remain extant. Seven of these can be extracted from the {{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}, and additional fragments of two articles have been collected since the early modern period. Modern editions, including those from the {{ill|National Historical Series|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historical Series|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}}, incorporate these reconstructed texts.

Since the {{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}} has been lost, the ''Ryō no Gige'' serves as a critical source for the transmission of both the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}} and the Yōrō Code<ref name=":0" />. Unlike the ''{{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}'', a private commentary compiled by {{ill|Wemune Naomoto|ja|惟宗直本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemune Naomoto|1=Koremune no Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492956}}, the ''Ryō no Gige'' was an officially sanctioned commentary with legal authority.

The structure of the text presents the original law in bold characters, with the interpretive commentary added in smaller, double-spaced text between the lines.

== Compilers ==
* Hanbe no Matsunaga (漢部松長) – {{ill|Daishoi|ja|大初位|1=Senior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Myōbōtokugyōshō|ja|明法得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōbōtokugyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kawakare Katsunari (川枯勝成) – {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} U11er, Minor Official of {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice}}
* {{ill|Sanuki no Emanari|ja|讃岐永直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki no Emanari|1=Sanuki no Naganao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632551}} – {{ill|Jūrokui|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūrokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Left Recorder, {{ill|Myōbōhakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōbōhakase|1=myōbō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512424}}
* {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura}} – {{ill|Jūgoin|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoin|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Minor Governor of {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}}
* Zendo no Masada (善道真貞) – {{ill|Shōgoi|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Governor of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province}}
* Okiwara Toshihisa (興原敏久) – {{ill|Shōgoi|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} U11er, Senior Judge of {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice}}
* Fujiwara no Mamoru (藤原衛) – {{ill|Jūshii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Vice Minister of {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice}}
* Fujiwara no Uton (藤原雄敏) – {{ill|Jūshii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Senior Official of {{ill|Kageyushō|ja|勘解由使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kageyushō|1=Kageyushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10901369}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|en|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|WD=Q3503077|lt=Sugawara no Kiyokimi}} – {{ill|Shōshii|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Left Kyoto District Governor|ja|左京大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Kyoto District Governor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京職|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}}
* {{ill|Fujiwara no Tsunetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tsunetsugu|WD=Q5507547|lt=Fujiwara no Tsunatsugu}} – {{ill|Jūshii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}
* Minabuchi no Hirozane (南淵弘貞) – {{ill|Jūsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, Councilor
* {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|1=Kiyohara no Natsuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560500}} – {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[it:Ryō no Gige]][[ja:令義解]][[ko:영의해]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[it:Ryō no Gige]][[ja:令義解]][[ko:영의해]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[it:Ryō no Gige]][[ja:令義解]][[ko:영의해]]

{{Nihon no hō}}
'''Ryō no Gige''' (令義解) is an interpretive text of the {{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}, created to establish precise guidelines<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|和書|title=令義解 凡例|year=1939|publisher=新訂増補 国史大系 第22巻|page=1|author=黒板|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431635/1/108}}</ref>. It was compiled in 833 CE ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō}} 10) under an imperial edict from Emperor Junna, with the right minister {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|1=Kiyohara no Natsuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560500}} serving as chief editor. The compilation involved 12 notable scholars, including {{ill|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|en|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|WD=Q3503077|lt=Sugawara no Kiyotomo}}, {{ill|Sanuki no Emanari|ja|讃岐永直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki no Emanari|1=Sanuki no Naganao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632551}}, and others<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=令義解序|publisher= |series=新訂増補 国史大系|volume=第22|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431635/1/118|author=黒板}}</ref>. The work originally comprised 30 articles across 10 volumes<ref name=":0" />, but only 21 articles remain extant. Seven of these can be extracted from the {{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}, and additional fragments of two articles have been collected since the early modern period. Modern editions, including those from the {{ill|National Historical Series|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historical Series|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}}, incorporate these reconstructed texts.

Since the {{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}} has been lost, the ''Ryō no Gige'' serves as a critical source for the transmission of both the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code}} and the Yōrō Code<ref name=":0" />. Unlike the ''{{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}'', a private commentary compiled by {{ill|Wemune Naomoto|ja|惟宗直本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemune Naomoto|1=Koremune no Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492956}}, the ''Ryō no Gige'' was an officially sanctioned commentary with legal authority.

The structure of the text presents the original law in bold characters, with the interpretive commentary added in smaller, double-spaced text between the lines.

== Compilers ==
* Hanbe no Matsunaga (漢部松長) – {{ill|Daishoi|ja|大初位|1=Senior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Myōbōtokugyōshō|ja|明法得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōbōtokugyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kawakare Katsunari (川枯勝成) – {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} U11er, Minor Official of {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice}}
* {{ill|Sanuki no Emanari|ja|讃岐永直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki no Emanari|1=Sanuki no Naganao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632551}} – {{ill|Jūrokui|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūrokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Left Recorder, {{ill|Myōbōhakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōbōhakase|1=myōbō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512424}}
* {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura}} – {{ill|Jūgoin|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoin|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Minor Governor of {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu}}
* Zendo no Masada (善道真貞) – {{ill|Shōgoi|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Governor of {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province}}
* Okiwara Toshihisa (興原敏久) – {{ill|Shōgoi|ja|正五位|1=Senior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōgoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fifth Rank|qq=}} U11er, Senior Judge of {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice}}
* Fujiwara no Mamoru (藤原衛) – {{ill|Jūshii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Vice Minister of {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice}}
* Fujiwara no Uton (藤原雄敏) – {{ill|Jūshii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Senior Official of {{ill|Kageyushō|ja|勘解由使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kageyushō|1=Kageyushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10901369}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|en|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|WD=Q3503077|lt=Sugawara no Kiyokimi}} – {{ill|Shōshii|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Left Kyoto District Governor|ja|左京大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Kyoto District Governor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京職|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}}
* {{ill|Fujiwara no Tsunetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tsunetsugu|WD=Q5507547|lt=Fujiwara no Tsunatsugu}} – {{ill|Jūshii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi}}
* Minabuchi no Hirozane (南淵弘貞) – {{ill|Jūsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, Councilor
* {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|1=Kiyohara no Natsuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560500}} – {{ill|Shōsanmi|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}, {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|WD=Q1351174|lt=Udaijin}}

Seven of these can be extracted from the {{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}, and additional fragments of two articles have been collected since the early modern period. Modern editions, including those from the {{ill|National Historical Series|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historical Series|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}}, incorporate these reconstructed texts.

Since the {{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}} has been lost, the ''Ryō no Gige'' serves as a critical source for the transmission of both the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}} and the Yōrō Code<ref name=":0" />. Unlike the ''{{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}'', a private commentary compiled by {{ill|Wemune Naomoto|ja|惟宗直本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemune Naomoto|1=Koremune no Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492956}}, the ''Ryō no Gige'' was an officially sanctioned commentary with legal authority.

The structure of the text presents the original law in bold characters, with the interpretive commentary added in smaller, double-spaced text between the lines.

{{Nihon no hō}}
'''Ryō no Gige''' (令義解) is an interpretive text of the {{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}}, created to establish precise guidelines<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|和書|title=令義解 凡例|year=1939|publisher=新訂増補 国史大系 第22巻|page=1|author=黒板|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431635/1/108}}</ref>. It was compiled in 833 CE ({{ill|Tenchō|en|Tenchō|WD=Q1373138|lt=Tenchō|1=Tenchō}} 10) under an imperial edict from Emperor Junna, with the right minister {{ill|Kiyohara no Natsuno|ja|清原夏野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyohara no Natsuno|1=Kiyohara no Natsuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560500}} serving as chief editor. The compilation involved 12 notable scholars, including {{ill|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|en|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|WD=Q3503077|lt=Sugawara no Kiyotomo|1=Sugawara no Kiyotomo}}, {{ill|Sanuki no Emanari|ja|讃岐永直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki no Emanari|1=Sanuki no Naganao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632551}}, and others<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=令義解序|publisher= |series=新訂増補 国史大系|volume=第22|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431635/1/118|author=黒板}}</ref>. The work originally comprised 30 articles across 10 volumes<ref name=":0" />, but only 21 articles remain extant. Seven of these can be extracted from the {{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}, and additional fragments of two articles have been collected since the early modern period. Modern editions, including those from the {{ill|National Historical Series|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historical Series|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}}, incorporate these reconstructed texts.

Since the {{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}} has been lost, the ''Ryō no Gige'' serves as a critical source for the transmission of both the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}} and the Yōrō Code<ref name=":0" />. Unlike the ''{{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}'', a private commentary compiled by {{ill|Wemune Naomoto|ja|惟宗直本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemune Naomoto|1=Koremune no Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492956}}, the ''Ryō no Gige'' was an officially sanctioned commentary with legal authority.

The structure of the text presents the original law in bold characters, with the interpretive commentary added in smaller, double-spaced text between the lines.

== Compilers ==
* Hanbe no Matsunaga (漢部松長) – {{ill|Daishoi|ja|大初位|1=Senior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Myōbōtokugyōshō|ja|明法得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōbōtokugyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* Kawakare Katsunari (川枯勝成) – {{ill|Jūhachi-i|ja|従八位|1=Junior Eighth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūhachi-i|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Eighth Rank|qq=}} U11er, Minor Official of {{ill|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|en|Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)|WD=Q6867320|lt=Ministry of Justice|1=Ministry of Justice (pre-modern Japan)}}
* {{ill|Sanuki no Emanari|ja|讃岐永直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki no Emanari|1=Sanuki no Naganao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632551}} – {{ill|Jūrokui|ja|従六位|1=Junior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūrokui|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Sixth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Left Recorder, {{ill|Myōbōhakase|ja|明法博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōbōhakase|1=Myōhō Hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512424}}

* Fujiwara no Uton (藤原雄敏) – {{ill|Jūshii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Senior Official of {{ill|Kageyushō|ja|勘解由使|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kageyushō|1=Kageyushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10901369}}
* {{ill|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|en|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|WD=Q3503077|lt=Sugawara no Kiyokimi|1=Sugawara no Kiyotomo}} – {{ill|Shōshii|ja|正四位|1=Senior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Left Kyoto District Governor|ja|左京大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Left Kyoto District Governor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 京職|1=Right Capital Ward Officer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374719}}
* {{ill|Fujiwara no Tsunetsugu|en|Fujiwara no Tsunetsugu|WD=Q5507547|lt=Fujiwara no Tsunatsugu|1=Fujiwara no Tsunetsugu}} – {{ill|Jūshii|ja|従四位|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshii|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Sangi (Japan)|en|Sangi (Japan)|WD=Q1133663|lt=Sangi|1=Sangi (Japan)}}
* Minabuchi no Hirozane (南淵弘貞) – {{ill|Jūsanmi|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūsanmi|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}}, Councilor

The compilation involved 12 notable scholars, including {{ill|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|en|Sugawara no Kiyotomo|WD=Q3503077|lt=Sugawara no Kiyotomo|1=Sugawara no Kiyotomo}}, {{ill|Sanuki no Emanari|ja|讃岐永直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki no Emanari|1=Sanuki no Naganao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11632551}}, and others<ref>{{Cite book|和書|title=令義解序|publisher= |series=新訂増補 国史大系|volume=第22|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3431635/1/118|author=黒板}}</ref>. The work originally comprised 30 articles across 10 volumes<ref name=":0" />, but only 21 articles remain extant. Seven of these can be extracted from the {{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}, and additional fragments of two articles have been collected since the early modern period. Modern editions, including those from the {{ill|National Historical Series|ja|国史大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Historical Series|1=Kokushi Taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10926780}}, incorporate these reconstructed texts.

Since the {{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōrō Code|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 養老律令|1=Yōrō Code|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2575743}} has been lost, the ''Ryō no Gige'' serves as a critical source for the transmission of both the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Code|1=Taihō Code}} and the Yōrō Code<ref name=":0" />. Unlike the ''{{ill|Ryōshū Gige|ja|令集解|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōshū Gige|1=Ryōshūge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378321}}'', a private commentary compiled by {{ill|Wemune Naomoto|ja|惟宗直本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wemune Naomoto|1=Koremune no Naomoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492956}}, the ''Ryō no Gige'' was an officially sanctioned commentary with legal authority.

The structure of the text presents the original law in bold characters, with the interpretive commentary added in smaller, double-spaced text between the lines.

* Hanbe no Matsunaga (漢部松長) – {{ill|Daishoi|ja|大初位|1=Senior Initial Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daishoi|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Initial Rank|qq=}} Lower, {{ill|Myōbōtokugyōshō|ja|明法得業生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōbōtokugyōshō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}

* {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura|1=Ono no Takamura}} – {{ill|Jūgoin|ja|従五位|1=Junior Fifth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoin|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|qq=}} Lower, Minor Governor of {{ill|Dazaifu (government)|en|Dazaifu (government)|WD=Q144845|lt=Dazaifu|1=Dazaifu (government)}}
